You are the native CEO/spec author for Phase 2 of the switchyard roadmap release train. Do not use scripts/codex-org. Work only in this phase worktree: /Users/vasuyadav/Downloads/Projects/switchyard/.worktrees/native-roadmap-20260529/phase-2-r3-runtime-capability-infrastructure. Do not touch the root checkout.

Goal: write the Phase 2 spec for R3 Runtime Capability Infrastructure, based on PRODUCT.md. This is a native TUI workflow; no PR creation. The spec should be concrete enough for a CTO to write a task graph and for one implementer/reviewer/auditor loop to execute the phase.

Phase metadata:
- Phase no: 2
- Roadmap release: R3: Runtime Capability Infrastructure
- Branch: agent/phase-2-r3-runtime-capability-infrastructure
- Previous phase head: agent/phase-1-r0-r2-verification-and-release-truth-reconciliation at 6687954e2c7361ba2eb14b31182c4e46d0fb358d
- Spec target path: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-29-phase-2-r3-runtime-capability-infrastructure.md

R3 roadmap from PRODUCT.md:
Goal: model runtimes, runtime modes, provider capabilities, and adapter health before adding more adapters.
Why: Today Codex exec --json exists, but that is not the same as full Codex runtime support. Future providers expose different modes: one-shot process, interactive process, PTY, ACP, SDK, HTTP wrapper, browser-backed, hosted-safe, and local-only. Switchyard needs a capability model before more adapters are connected.
Release scope:
- runtime mode vocabulary, for example codex.exec_json, future codex.interactive, opencode.acp, agentfield.async_rest.
- adapter manifest shape for capabilities and limitations.
- doctor/check service that reports version, availability, auth/config state, and partial support.
- registry schema/API updates for capabilities.
- placement facts for local, hosted, and future hybrid execution.
- clear distinction between provider, runtime, runtime mode, model, and adapter.
- update Codex exec-json registration to use the new vocabulary.
Usable after release:
- runtime mode vocabulary such as codex.exec_json versus future codex.interactive.
- adapter manifest shape.
- doctor/check service.
- capability records in registry.
- placement facts for local, hosted, and future hybrid execution.
- runtime availability API that distinguishes installed, unavailable, unsupported, and partially supported runtimes.
- owners and clients can ask what this Switchyard instance can actually run.
Not included:
- full interactive Codex runtime.
- broad adapter expansion.
Local verification:
- registry and/or doctor API shows codex.exec_json as available when Codex is installed.
- same API marks Codex unavailable without crashing when Codex is missing or model probing fails.
- fake runtime reports deterministic capabilities.
- existing run creation still works after registry/capability changes.
Promotion criteria:
- no runtime is described as broadly supported unless a specific runtime mode is actually implemented.
- every adapter added later must declare capabilities through this mechanism.

Spec requirements:
- Inspect existing code/docs enough to stay consistent with current packages and REST style.
- Define endpoint/API contract for runtime/capability/doctor inspection without over-scoping R4+.
- Define data model/schema expectations, adapter manifest shape, error behavior, and migration/backward compatibility constraints.
- Define test cases and local verification commands.
- Write the spec file at the target path. If docs/superpowers/specs is ignored, still write the file; the orchestrator will force-add it later if needed.
- Commit your spec on the phase branch with message: docs: add phase 2 r3 capability spec

Final response: JSON-like status GREEN/BLOCKED, spec_path, commit hash, files changed, and any decisions/risks.

Goal: write the Phase 3 spec for R4 Shared Runtime Substrates And Generic HTTP, based on PRODUCT.md and the current code after R3. This is a native TUI workflow; no PR creation. The spec must be concrete enough for CTO plan + implementer/reviewer/auditor loop.

Phase metadata:
- Phase no: 3
- Roadmap release: R4: Shared Runtime Substrates And Generic HTTP
- Branch: agent/phase-3-r4-shared-runtime-substrates-and-generic-http
- Previous phase head: agent/phase-2-r3-runtime-capability-infrastructure at e0fc7ffdf0d5b1d06da2bd6fc07e474e30895aa8
- Spec target path: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-29-phase-3-r4-shared-runtime-substrates-and-generic-http.md

R4 roadmap from PRODUCT.md:
Goal: extract reusable runtime infrastructure from the proven Codex slice, then validate a second adapter shape through Generic HTTP.
Why: Provider infrastructure will vary, but adapters still need common behavior: lifecycle, cancellation, timeout, event normalization, transcript capture, artifact persistence, health checks, and capability reporting. Codex proved a process-backed shape. Generic HTTP proves a wrapper-backed shape without forcing ACP complexity yet.
Release scope:
- shared process-runner substrate extracted from Codex without weakening Codex behavior.
- transcript capture standard across adapters.
- stdout/stderr/event parser harness.
- cancellation and timeout helpers.
- stronger adapter contract tests that every adapter must satisfy.
- Generic HTTP adapter for simple wrapper runtimes.
- fake HTTP wrapper test server for deterministic CI coverage.
- adapter docs template updated to match the capability model.
Usable after release:
- shared process-runner substrate for process-backed adapters.
- transcript capture standard.
- stdout/stderr/event parser harness.
- cancellation and timeout helpers.
- stronger adapter contract tests.
- Generic HTTP adapter for simple wrapper runtimes.
- Switchyard can run one process-backed provider mode and one HTTP-wrapper-backed provider mode through the same public run lifecycle.
Not included:
- ACP/acpx.
- PTY.
- full Codex interactive mode.
Local verification:
- Codex exec-json behavior still passes all existing tests and smoke checks after substrate extraction.
- Generic HTTP adapter can run against a local fake HTTP runtime.
- Generic HTTP status, failure, cancellation, output, and artifact paths are normalized.
- transcript artifacts are stored for both process and HTTP adapter paths.
Promotion criteria:
- new adapter work no longer copies Codex-specific process code.
- adapter contract tests are reusable by process and HTTP adapters.

Spec requirements:
- Inspect current code/docs after R3 to stay consistent with runtime modes, doctor/check, adapter manifests, storage and REST patterns.
- Define Generic HTTP runtime mode(s), manifest/capabilities, env/config model, request/response contract, timeout/cancel semantics, event/artifact normalization, error behavior, and fake HTTP test server boundaries.
- Define process substrate extraction expectations without changing public Codex behavior.
- Define test cases and local verification commands.
- Keep scope to R4. Do not build ACP, PTY, interactive Codex, hosted, SDK/CLI, debate, tools/memory/approval.
- Write the spec file at the target path and commit it with message: docs: add phase 3 r4 generic http spec

Final response: JSON-like status GREEN/BLOCKED, spec_path, commit hash, files changed, decisions, risks.

Phase 3 reviewer returned NEEDS_REVISION. Please fix the single major finding in the same Phase 3 worktree/branch and commit a focused revision. Do not use scripts/codex-org. Work only in /Users/vasuyadav/Downloads/Projects/switchyard/.worktrees/native-roadmap-20260529/phase-3-r4-shared-runtime-substrates-and-generic-http. Do not revert the implementation commit e473291724491d6d132823a1055b0319c1634837.

Finding:
- File: packages/adapters/src/generic-http/generic-http-adapter.ts around line 450
- Issue: Status endpoint failures are swallowed as `running` during Generic HTTP polling. `fetchStatus()` catches every error and returns `running`, so when `/events` is empty the adapter can loop until runner timeout instead of failing with required status-specific reason codes. Reviewer reproduced custom fetch where `/events` returned [], `/status` returned invalid JSON, and `adapter.events().next()` timed out after 250ms while polling 21 times instead of yielding `run.failed`.
- Required fix: Change `fetchStatus()` to propagate status-response protocol errors distinctly instead of collapsing them to `running`. In `events()`, map invalid status to `generic_http_invalid_status_response` and oversized status to `generic_http_status_response_too_large` when status fallback is used after empty events or terminal=true without terminal event.
- Add adapter tests for empty-events + invalid status and empty-events + oversized status.

Run at least:
- pnpm --filter @switchyard/adapters test
- pnpm typecheck
- pnpm test
- git diff --check

Commit message: `fix: surface generic http status fallback errors`

Return JSON-like GREEN/BLOCKED with commit hash, changed files, checks, and how the finding was resolved.

Goal: add the structured ACP/acpx path and validate it with OpenCode.
Why: ACP is a core target protocol for structured coding runtimes. It deserves its own foundation before OpenCode is treated as just another process. OpenCode is the first real ACP candidate because local verification already proved initialization and session creation.

- packages/protocol-acpx.
- JSON-RPC framing and error handling.
- raw protocol transcript artifact support.
- fake ACP runtime harness.
- outbound ACP client foundation.
- mapping ACP session updates to Switchyard events.
- OpenCode doctor.
- OpenCode ACP adapter.
- local OpenCode smoke docs.
Usable after release:
- packages/protocol-acpx.
- JSON-RPC framing and contract tests.
- fake ACP runtime harness.
- outbound ACP client foundation.
- OpenCode ACP adapter.
- raw ACP transcript artifacts.
- Switchyard can run a local OpenCode-backed task through the same run API.
Not included:
- hosted node connectivity.
- debate orchestration.
- full inbound ACP server unless explicitly included in the release spec.
Local verification:
- fake ACP runtime contract tests pass.
- OpenCode doctor reports binary/version and availability.
- OpenCode run produces normalized events.
- OpenCode cancellation maps to the run lifecycle.
- raw ACP transcript artifact is stored.
Promotion criteria:
- ACP code lives behind protocol boundaries and does not leak OpenCode-specific assumptions into core.
- OpenCode is implemented as an adapter over the ACP foundation, not as a bespoke process parser.

Spec requirements:
- Inspect current code/docs after R4 to stay consistent with runtime modes, adapter manifests, Generic HTTP/process substrates, runner/artifact behavior, and daemon wiring.

- Phase no: 5

R6 roadmap from PRODUCT.md:
Goal: prove Switchyard can coordinate a real wrapper runtime, not only local CLIs.
Why: Switchyard is meant to normalize direct runtimes and wrapper runtimes. Generic HTTP proves the wrapper contract in isolation; AgentField proves it against a real wrapper-style provider. This should happen before OpenClaw or Paperclip because AgentField has a clearer verified path.
Release scope:
- AgentField adapter using async REST execution.
- create-execution, poll/status, result, and failure mapping.
- wrapper execution metadata preserved in events and artifacts.
- wrapper capability and doctor reporting.
- local or mocked verification path that does not require uncontrolled spend.
- docs for AgentField setup and local verification.
Usable after release:
- AgentField adapter using async REST execution.
- create/poll/result/failure mapping.
- wrapper execution metadata and artifacts.
- wrapper-specific doctor and local verification docs.
- an AgentField workflow can be launched as a Switchyard run when configured.
Not included:
- OpenClaw or Paperclip until their API boundaries are verified.
- hosted worker execution.
- debate orchestration.
Local verification:
- AgentField doctor reports configured/unconfigured states clearly.
- mocked AgentField flow passes CI.
- approved local smoke can create an execution, poll it, and normalize completion/failure.
- artifacts preserve wrapper result payloads.
Promotion criteria:
- wrapper adapters follow Generic HTTP/runtime capability conventions.
- AgentField does not become a second control plane inside Switchyard.

Spec requirements:
- Inspect current code/docs after R5 to stay consistent with Generic HTTP, OpenCode, runtime modes, doctor/check, runner/artifact behavior, and daemon wiring.
- Define AgentField runtime mode, manifest/capabilities, env/config, API/request/response contract, mocked verification boundary, event/artifact normalization, failure/cancel behavior, doctor semantics, tests, docs.
- Keep scope R6 only. Do not implement OpenClaw, Paperclip, hosted workers, debate, broad tool/memory/approval expansion, SDK/CLI product.
- Avoid uncontrolled spend by making mocked/fake verification mandatory and real AgentField smoke optional/manual.
- Write spec file and commit with message: docs: add phase 5 r6 wrapper integration spec

Reviewer revision 1 for Phase 9 R10. Please fix in the same worktree/branch and commit a revision. Do not invoke scripts/codex-org.

Worktree: /Users/vasuyadav/Downloads/Projects/switchyard/.worktrees/native-roadmap-20260529/phase-9-r10-hosted-and-hybrid-execution
Branch: agent/phase-9-r10-hosted-and-hybrid-execution
Current implementation commit: 9f5fe5a540b6815c444a999bb9a4d056787bdd11

Findings to resolve:
1. HIGH: Hosted worker retry semantics are broken. `handleRunFailure()` calls queue.retry then queue.fail, leaving job failed and not claimable. Separate retry bookkeeping from terminal failure. Non-exhausted failure must leave job claimable; only exhausted failures terminalize run/job. Add focused test: startRun fails once, same job can be claimed and completed on next worker tick.
2. HIGH: Connected node fabricates a hard-coded Run after claiming an assignment. Extend node assignment flow so claimed assignment includes authoritative run payload or fetch path, then apps/node enforces policy and executes against durable run record. Node should sync real emitted events/artifacts, not empty arrays, before completing assignment.
3. HIGH: Code/docs mismatch for Postgres/Redis/object store. Current classes are memory wrappers and server never wires real service config. Either implement advertised opt-in real-service wiring end-to-end or explicitly downgrade shipped scope/docs/product/changelog to deterministic fake/memory surfaces. Given time and default verification constraints, the pragmatic path is to downgrade product/docs truth and class names if needed so R10 honestly ships production-shaped interfaces/memory substitutes, not real Postgres/Redis/object storage. Do not overclaim actual pg/Redis/S3 backing unless implemented.
4. HIGH: Event sync must reject events whose runId is missing/wrong for assignment.runId and validate input.cursor against assignment.lastEventSequence before accepting. Add negative tests for wrong-run events and stale/conflicting cursor retries.

Also rerun focused package tests and typecheck/full tests if practical. Return JSON with status GREEN or DONE_WITH_CONCERNS, commit, changed_files, checks, summary, concerns.

Phase 9 auditor pass 1 returned NEEDS_REVISION. Please fix these redflags in the same worktree/branch, on top of current HEAD 3f37371d9fe5284f8da43b67026be9c7bd5e12ee, and commit a revision. Do not invoke scripts/codex-org and do not merge elsewhere.

Worktree: /Users/vasuyadav/Downloads/Projects/switchyard/.worktrees/native-roadmap-20260529/phase-9-r10-hosted-and-hybrid-execution
Branch: agent/phase-9-r10-hosted-and-hybrid-execution
Audit report: agent-runs/native-roadmap-20260529/audit/phase-9-report.md

Redflags to fix:
1. HIGH: Hosted enqueue failure leaves orphaned hosted queued run after returning queue_unavailable. In packages/core/src/services/hosted-run-service.ts, if enqueue fails after durable run/placement creation, update run to failed, append a named failure event/reason (queue_enqueue_failed or queue_unavailable as appropriate), and keep placement/job state consistent. Only return queue_unavailable with no side effects when failure occurs before durable creation. Add regression test that reproduces enqueue failure and asserts run is terminal failed, not queued.
2. HIGH: Event sync accepts events for pending assignments. In packages/core/src/services/event-sync-service.ts, reject sync unless assignment status is an allowed in-flight state such as claimed or running. Add regression tests for pending, terminal, and wrong-owner/wrong-state sync attempts.
3. HIGH: Real opt-in hosted infra wiring is still missing where spec/plan acceptance requires it. Implement real opt-in wiring end-to-end enough to satisfy the acceptance, while keeping deterministic memory substitutes by default:
   - apps/server config must parse Postgres/Redis/object-store env vars and choose real implementations only when configured.
   - apps/server/app and apps/worker must wire real Postgres/Redis/object-store implementations when config is present; memory substitutes remain default.
   - packages/queue/src/bullmq-run-queue.ts must either use BullMQ when Redis config is present or be honestly renamed/downgraded. Since the spec asks Redis/BullMQ opt-in, prefer implementing a real BullMQ-backed adapter with opt-in tests skipped when Redis env is absent.
   - packages/storage/src/postgres/* must use pg/Drizzle or equivalent real Postgres connection when configured, not Map wrappers, at least for the stores advertised as opt-in. If full implementation is too large, narrow to the run/event/session/artifact/registry/placement/node/assignment stores required by R10 and leave unsupported methods explicit.
   - object artifact store must have a real opt-in implementation using a named dependency or a documented filesystem/object-compatible local implementation if no network service is available. Avoid overclaiming S3/R2 if not implemented.
   - Update docs/product/changelog truth to match the final code exactly.

Run the relevant focused tests and full `pnpm test`/`pnpm typecheck` if practical. Return JSON with status GREEN or DONE_WITH_CONCERNS, commit, changed_files, checks, summary, and concerns.

Findings to fix:

Implement the required R11 command surface/flags and tests: `doctor --json --active-fake-check`, `daemon start --host --port --data-dir --artifact-dir --foreground --ready-timeout-ms`, fake run flags `--cwd --task --timeout-seconds`, debug flags `--include-artifact-content --live`, and support the spec/plan `runtimes test` command while preserving compatibility if needed. Expand tests for JSON/human output, invalid args, port/readiness failure, fake-run/debug SDK flows.
2. Packaging acceptance: package manifests currently export `src`. Switch public packages to built `dist` exports/bin where appropriate, ensure build emits dist, and add a root or package `release:smoke-local` clean-temp smoke that installs/uses local built artifacts outside the workspace. It must prove SDK import, CLI help/contract export, daemon startup/doctor/fake run/event replay/artifact content/shutdown as far as local deterministic tests allow.
3. `packages/adapters/src/compatibility-matrix.ts`: replace hard-coded thin rows with manifest-driven CI-safe matrix fields: adapter/provider/runtime ids, runtime mode id/slug, adapter type/kind, capabilities, limitations, placement support, doctor strategy, no-spend harness type, covered scenarios, and CI status. Validate missing manifests/harness/scenario coverage.
4. `packages/contracts/src/endpoint-inventory.ts` and `openapi.ts`: add descriptor fields (`surface`, `operationId`, query schema, request body schema, explicit no-body marker, success schema details, error-envelope ownership), and generate less-placeholder OpenAPI from real contract schemas, including request/query/body, surface tags, SSE, raw artifact content, and common error envelope.
5. `apps/daemon/src/app.ts`: honor bounded inbound `x-request-id`, generate otherwise, reject/normalize invalid headers, and test both accepted and rejected header values.
6. `packages/storage/src/sqlite/database.hardening.test.ts`: add representative SQL snapshot/fixture matrix for pre-R3, pre-R7, pre-R9, and pre-R11 shapes and verify open/reopen preservation, schema version recording, and row survival across named tables.

Task: P12-T1-s3-r2-object-store-client-wiring
Current implementation commit: d143f071fc2d14e6bdb632ea8a55c8fbbef200d0

Reviewer returned NEEDS_REVISION with two HIGH blockers. Fix only these issues and any tests needed:

1. Size-based content integrity mismatch mapping is wrong. In packages/storage/src/object-artifact-content-store.ts and packages/storage/src/local-object-artifact-content-store.ts, metadata sizeBytes mismatches must be integrity failures mapping to 409, not object_store_read_failed/object_store_unavailable 503. Keep artifact_content_empty only for expected > 0 and actual === 0 if useful; all other size mismatches should use an integrity code already mapped to 409, likely artifact_digest_mismatch. Update packages/storage/test/artifact-content-store.test.ts and protocol-rest tests as needed.

2. SWITCHYARD_OBJECT_STORE_PROBE=disabled must be honored for every backend in local/test. Server readiness currently always probes s3-compatible and worker readiness has backend !== "memory" || probe !== "disabled". Change server readiness to skip probe when config.objectStore.probe === "disabled" for local and s3-compatible; change worker readiness to gate probing on probe !== "disabled". Add tests proving disabled mode performs no probe call for local and s3-compatible readiness.

Run at least:
- pnpm --filter @switchyard/storage test
- pnpm --filter @switchyard/protocol-rest test
- pnpm --filter @switchyard/server test
- pnpm --filter @switchyard/worker test
- pnpm typecheck
- git diff --check

Commit before returning GREEN. Return status, commit hash, changed files, checks, summary, concerns.

Current head: b7cbca73b0e98c8babb81cba3dbc505e5391614a

Auditor returned NEEDS_REVISION with two HIGH redflags. Fix exactly these and add tests:

1. packages/storage/src/object-store-config.ts: local/test S3-compatible endpoint validation accepts arbitrary HTTP hosts. Contract says HTTP is allowed only for localhost, 127.0.0.1, and intended Docker-network cases. Auditor direct probe showed http://evil.example.com and http://minio accepted. Tighten validation. A conservative acceptable rule: in local/test allow http only for localhost, 127.0.0.1, ::1, and docker-network hostnames that are explicitly opted in via a new/env existing safe mechanism if already planned; if no explicit mechanism exists, reject arbitrary hostnames like minio too. Keep https accepted. Add tests rejecting http://evil.example.com and http://minio in local/test while accepting http://127.0.0.1:9000 and localhost.

2. packages/storage/src/s3-compatible-object-client.ts: GetObject total timeout only wraps SDK send(); body conversion/draining can hang indefinitely. Move body conversion inside the same total timeout envelope or add a second bounded timeout that still honors requestTimeoutMs for the full GetObject operation. Add fake-client test where send returns a body getReader().read() that never resolves and verify getObject rejects with object_store_timeout within configured timeout.

- git diff --check

6. Add registry availability wording so server-seeded real hosted modes do not imply server-local provider execution or managed production support.
7. Add deployment rollback/runbook notes: disable gate or remove real modes from allowlist; queued real jobs fail closed at worker claim revalidation; alert on failures/timeouts/unsupported interactions/object-store failures/retry exhaustion.

Do not touch root checkout. Do not merge branches. Keep scope focused.

Reviewer finding to fix:
HIGH: `apps/worker/src/hosted-runtime-adapters.ts` hosted-safe logger preserves short provider output. `CodexExecJsonAdapter` logs stderr under `{ text: ... }`; current sanitizer only replaces long `text`, so short stderr/stdout/provider output can leak. R15 requires hosted-safe logging to redact raw stderr/stdout/task/cwd/argv/provider output/tokens/signed URLs/object keys.

Required changes:
1. Tighten `createHostedSafeLogger` so provider output is always redacted for keys representing stdout/stderr/text/output/provider output/task/cwd/argv/args/command args/env/tokens/signed URLs/object keys. Include snake_case variants like `signed_url` and `object_key`.
2. Add/extend worker regression tests proving hosted adapter logs do not expose raw short stderr/stdout/provider output and do redact signed_url/object_key variants.
3. Run focused checks:
   - `pnpm --filter @switchyard/worker test`
   - `pnpm typecheck`
   - `git diff --check`
   Add broader checks if your change requires them.
4. Commit with message `fix(hosted): redact hosted adapter provider output logs`.

Return status GREEN or BLOCKED, commit hash, files changed, checks run, and concerns.

`/Users/vasuyadav/Downloads/Projects/switchyard/.worktrees/post-r11-remaining-20260530/phase-14-r15-hosted-real-runtime-execution`
Branch: `agent/phase-14-r15-hosted-real-runtime-execution`

Do not touch root checkout. Do not merge branches. Keep scope focused.

Auditor redflag to fix:
CRITICAL acceptance bug: hosted worker durable-row preparation is not actually conditional. `packages/core/src/services/hosted-worker-service.ts` `persistPreparedRun()` does reread -> unconditional `runs.update(next)` -> reread. A concurrent mutation can be overwritten and `startRun()` can still fire. Auditor reproduced this with a custom RunStore that changes the row to `cancelled` immediately before metadata write; `started: 1` still happened.

Required change:
1. Implement a guarded compare-and-update for prepared metadata write.
   - If a storage port already has conditional update support, use it.
   - If not, extend the RunStore port and implement guarded update in sqlite/postgres/in-memory stores as needed, or add a narrow optional capability with a safe fallback that does not claim atomicity where unavailable.
   - The guard must ensure `id`, `status`, `placement`, `runtime`, `runtimeMode`, `provider`, and `adapterType` still match before writing prepared metadata.
2. Make `HostedWorkerService` use the guarded result before `startRun()`.
   - If guard fails, fail run/job with `hosted_run_state_invalid` and do not call `startRun()`.
3. Add regression coverage proving concurrent mutation yields `hosted_run_state_invalid` and no adapter start.
   - Include the auditor repro style with a custom RunStore or storage-level tests if needed.
4. Run at least:
   - `pnpm --filter @switchyard/core test`
   - `pnpm --filter @switchyard/storage test` if storage port implementation changes
   - `pnpm --filter @switchyard/server test` if interface changes affect server
   - `pnpm --filter @switchyard/worker test` if interface changes affect worker
   - `pnpm typecheck`
   - `git diff --check`
5. Commit with message `fix(hosted): guard prepared hosted run updates`.

Return GREEN or BLOCKED, commit hash, files changed, checks run, and concerns.

You are the R16 implementer revision agent for Switchyard.

Workspace:
`/Users/vasuyadav/Downloads/Projects/switchyard/.worktrees/post-r11-remaining-20260530/phase-15-r16-interactive-codex-and-approval-bridges`
Branch: `agent/phase-15-r16-interactive-codex-and-approval-bridges`

Do not touch root checkout. Do not merge branches. Keep scope focused.

Reviewer blockers to fix:

1. HIGH quality: `packages/core/src/services/approval-service.ts` expired runtime approvals can hang late approve/reject. `resolve()` holds `withResolutionLock(id, ...)` and calls `transitionPendingRuntimeApproval()`, which re-enters the same lock. Timer path has same nested-lock pattern. Required fix: remove lock re-entry; factor pending->expired/rejected transition into internal helper that assumes the caller already holds the lock. Use it from `resolve()` and timer callback. Add regression test that advances injected clock past `expiresAt`, calls `approve()` and/or `reject()`, and asserts it returns promptly with `approval_not_pending`, approval status is `expired`, and expiration side effects fire exactly once.

2. HIGH spec: `packages/core/src/services/runtime-runner-service.ts` does not terminalize pending approvals when adapter emits terminal `run.failed` directly. Main event loop calls `terminalizeRunFromAdapterEvent()`, which updates run/session and appends event but does not call `terminalizeRuntimeApprovalsForRun()`. Required fix: terminalize linked pending runtime approvals when processing adapter-emitted `run.failed` and `run.cancelled` before returning. Add regression test where adapter emits `approval.requested` followed by `run.failed` and assert pending approval is terminalized with matching event/side effect.

Required checks:
- `pnpm --filter @switchyard/core test -- runtime-approval-session-r16`
- `pnpm --filter @switchyard/core test`
- `pnpm typecheck`
- `git diff --check`

Commit with message `fix(core): terminalize runtime approvals without lock reentry`.
Return GREEN or BLOCKED with commit hash, files changed, checks run, and concerns.

Audit report: agent-runs/post-r11-remaining-20260530/audit/phase-17-report.md

Fix exactly these audit redflags:

1) P17-T4 security blocker: hosted artifact content reads do not enforce tenant ownership before artifact existence/content-state branches. An authenticated cross-tenant caller can probe `GET /artifacts/:id/content` and learn an unowned artifact exists with `contentStored=false` before authorization runs.
- Files called out: packages/protocol-rest/src/artifact-routes.ts around lines 80/85/91.
- Required change: Move tenant/project authorization ahead of artifact existence and `contentStored` checks for `/artifacts/:id/content`, then add a regression test for an unowned artifact with `contentStored=false`.

2) P17-T2/T3/T6 acceptance blocker: quota reservations and audit events never receive durable `resource_ownership` rows, and readiness ignores their unowned counts. This violates phase acceptance requiring ownership for quota/audit resources and fail-closed readiness on unowned hosted resources.
- Files called out: packages/core/src/services/control-plane-service.ts, packages/storage/src/postgres/control-plane-store.ts, apps/server/src/readiness.ts.
- Required change: Attach `resource_ownership` rows when quota reservations and audit events are created, include audit events and quota reservations in the `/ready` unowned-resource gate, and add regression coverage proving readiness fails when either class is unowned.

Preserve all prior behavior and tests. Do not edit audit artifacts unless you need to mention fix context; implementation/source/tests should be enough.

Revision required for P18-T4-readiness-worker-node-operability. Same workspace/branch:
/Users/vasuyadav/Downloads/Projects/switchyard/.worktrees/post-r11-remaining-20260530/phase-18-r19-production-hosted-deployment

Reviewer found two blockers:

1. `runWorkerReadinessCommand()` can lose structured JSON on dependency failure. `worker.ready()` classifies unreachable Postgres as `{ ok:false, reason:'postgres_unavailable' }`, but the `finally { await worker.stop() }` path can re-await/rethrow the rejected postgresReady promise and turn the CLI into an uncaught `ECONNREFUSED` stack trace instead of structured redacted JSON. Make worker shutdown best-effort after readiness failure. `stop()` must not rethrow already-classified startup/readiness errors, or `runWorkerReadinessCommand()`/`main.ts` must swallow shutdown errors after failed readiness. Add regression coverage proving unreachable Postgres prints/returns structured JSON and exits nonzero with named reason, no stack trace.

2. `node.start_failed` can leak credentialed URLs. Production node config accepts `REDACTED`; then node startup logs `error.message` verbatim containing `REDACTED`. Fail closed on credential-bearing production node URLs and/or sanitize startup error messages before logging them. Add regression test proving `node.start_failed` never contains embedded credentials from `SWITCHYARD_SERVER_URL`.

Stay within owned files if possible:
- apps/worker/src/worker.ts
- apps/worker/src/ready.ts
- apps/worker/src/main.ts

/Users/vasuyadav/Downloads/Projects/switchyard/.worktrees/post-r11-remaining-20260530/phase-19-r20-production-subprocess-pty
Branch: agent/phase-19-r20-production-subprocess-pty

You are not alone in the codebase. Do not revert edits made by other agents or the user. Work with the current branch state. Dependencies are GREEN:
- P19-T1 commit f5b2909 contracts: add R20 sandbox command policy contract schemas
- P19-T2 commit b354b8f core: gate real sandbox execution with command policy

Task:
P19-T3-production-sandbox-executor: Add production process and PTY executor substrate.

Owned files:
- packages/adapters/src/sandbox/production-hosted-sandbox-executor.ts
- packages/adapters/src/index.ts
- packages/adapters/test/production-hosted-sandbox-executor.test.ts

Context files:
- docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-31-phase-19-r20-production-subprocess-pty.md
- docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-31-phase-19-r20-production-subprocess-pty.md
- PRODUCT.md
- packages/core/src/services/hosted-sandbox-service.ts
- packages/adapters/src/substrates/process-runner.ts if present
- packages/adapters/src/tools/local-process-tool-executor.ts
- packages/adapters/test/substrates.test.ts
- packages/adapters/test/real-tool-adapters.test.ts
- packages/adapters/src/index.ts

Instructions from plan:
Create a new adapter substrate, not a runtime adapter. Export ProductionHostedSandboxExecutor plus process and PTY factory interfaces from packages/adapters/src/index.ts. The executor implements HostedSandboxExecutorPort and requires options.resolvedCommand for real execution. Process execution uses node:child_process.spawn through an injectable factory, shell:false, stdio pipes, exact argv/cwd/env from resolvedCommand, bounded output collection, stdin write only when policy allowed, and AbortSignal kill handling. PTY execution uses an injectable ptyFactory; when absent, return status failed with sandbox_pty_unavailable. PTY driver spawn errors, close failures, stream/data errors, write failures, resize failures, and abort cleanup must return named low-cardinality sandbox reason codes and must never fall back to process execution. Stdin write failures in the process path must also map to named sandbox failure and clean up the child. Do not register runtime adapters, add public APIs, invoke provider CLIs, or import testkit from production source.

Acceptance:
- Process execution calls spawn with shell:false and policy-resolved executablePath, argv, cwd, env.
- Injection-like argv entries remain literal argv entries.
- Missing resolvedCommand fails before spawn.
- Spawn errors map to sandbox_spawn_failed.
- Non-zero close returns failed with exitCode and sandbox_process_failed.
- AbortSignal cancellation kills child and settles cleanly.
- Output collection is bounded.
- PTY without ptyFactory returns sandbox_pty_unavailable.
- PTY spawn, close, stream/data, write, resize, and abort cleanup failures return named sandbox failures and never fall back to process execution.
- Process stdin write failure kills/cleans up the child and returns a named sandbox failure.
- PTY fake driver receives input and resize frames in order.
- Executor is exported for worker construction but no runtime adapter map changes are made.

Required checks before GREEN:
- pnpm --filter @switchyard/adapters test -- production-hosted-sandbox-executor.test.ts substrates.test.ts
- pnpm --filter @switchyard/adapters typecheck
- git diff --check

Commit your changes before returning GREEN. Return JSON only with status, commit, checks, files_changed, and summary. If blocked, return BLOCKED with exact reason.

Reviewer returned NEEDS_REVISION for P19-T3. Please revise the same task in the same worktree/branch and commit the fix.

Blocking finding:
- packages/adapters/src/sandbox/production-hosted-sandbox-executor.ts around truncateUtf8: current Buffer.subarray(...).toString('utf8') can emit replacement character U+FFFD when cutting through a multibyte char, which re-encodes to 3 bytes and can exceed maxBytes. This violates bounded-output acceptance for process and PTY output.

Required change:
1. Replace truncation logic with byte-safe UTF-8 truncation that only keeps complete code points within the byte budget and never returns a string whose Buffer.byteLength(value, 'utf8') exceeds maxBytes.
2. Add regression coverage in packages/adapters/test/production-hosted-sandbox-executor.test.ts using multibyte stdout/PTY output to prove stdoutBytes, stderrBytes, and combinedOutputBytes are never exceeded.
3. Re-run required checks:
   - pnpm --filter @switchyard/adapters test -- production-hosted-sandbox-executor.test.ts substrates.test.ts
   - pnpm --filter @switchyard/adapters typecheck
   - git diff --check

Return JSON only with status, new commit, checks, files_changed, and summary.

---

Please re-review P19-T3 after implementer revision commit 5879f3a.

Revision summary:
- Replaced truncation with byte-safe UTF-8 truncation that iterates complete Unicode code points and stops before exceeding maxBytes.
- Added multibyte regression coverage for process and PTY output paths, including stdoutBytes, stderrBytes, and combinedOutputBytes assertions.
- Checks passed: pnpm --filter @switchyard/adapters test -- production-hosted-sandbox-executor.test.ts substrates.test.ts; pnpm --filter @switchyard/adapters typecheck; git diff --check.

Audit red-flag for your P19-T3 production sandbox executor. Please fix in the same worktree/branch and commit.

Workspace/worktree:
/Users/vasuyadav/Downloads/Projects/switchyard/.worktrees/post-r11-remaining-20260530/phase-19-r20-production-subprocess-pty
Branch: agent/phase-19-r20-production-subprocess-pty

Red flag:
- packages/adapters/src/sandbox/production-hosted-sandbox-executor.ts around process path lines ~153-179 and PTY path ~278-329: abort/cancel does not settle cleanly if the spawned process or PTY accepts SIGTERM (`kill()` returns true) but never emits `close`. The executor waits on `completion` forever. Auditor reproduced with fake process/PTY whose kill returned true but never emitted close.

Required fix:
1. Bound cleanup after abort and settle fail-closed even if `close` never arrives after SIGTERM, for both process and PTY execution paths.
2. Add regression tests in packages/adapters/test/production-hosted-sandbox-executor.test.ts for process abort-without-close and PTY abort-without-close. The executor must return a terminal cancelled or named failure result instead of hanging.
3. Keep previous behavior: normal close, spawn errors, nonzero, stdin failure, PTY missing/failures, UTF-8 byte-safe truncation still pass.

Required checks:
- pnpm --filter @switchyard/adapters test -- production-hosted-sandbox-executor.test.ts substrates.test.ts
- pnpm --filter @switchyard/adapters typecheck
- git diff --check

Return JSON only with status, new commit, checks, files_changed, and summary.

Instructions from plan:
Create a new contract module for R21 provider runtime activation. Define a closed provider runtime mode schema with exactly codex.exec_json, claude_code.sdk, and opencode.acp. Define providerRuntimePolicySchema for a JSON object { version: 1, modes: Record<known real mode, entry> } with strict object validation so unknown mode keys fail with provider_runtime_policy_unknown_mode. Each entry must include enabled, executablePath, cwdPrefixes, envAllowlist, requiredEnv, fixedArgs where applicable, allowUserArgs fixed false, spendControls, and provider-specific permission fields. Codex fixedArgs must be exactly ['exec','--json'] and sandbox must be read_only. Claude permissionMode must be read_only and disabledTools must include Bash, WebFetch, and WebSearch. OpenCode fixedArgs must be exactly ['acp'] and onePromptPerRun must be true. Credentials must be represented only as required env var names, never raw secret values. Add a providerResolvedCommandSchema for worker-owned adapter handoff with runtimeMode, executablePath, argv, cwd, env, envKeys, allowUserArgs false, and redactedSummary. Add a providerRuntimeFailureCodeSchema covering every named code in the R21 spec. Export inferred types through packages/contracts/src/index.ts. Do not modify the generic sandbox contract or remove the R20 provider executable denylist.

Acceptance:
- Valid policies parse for codex.exec_json, claude_code.sdk, and opencode.acp with provider-specific required fields.
- Unknown provider mode keys, fake.deterministic entries, Cursor/OpenClaw/Paperclip entries, and generic process/PTY entries are rejected.
- Entries with allowUserArgs true, relative executablePath, empty cwdPrefixes, relative cwdPrefixes, invalid env var names, missing spendControls, or raw credential-like fields are rejected.
- Codex policy rejects non-read-only sandbox values or fixedArgs other than ['exec','--json'].
- Claude policy rejects permissionMode values other than read_only and rejects missing Bash, WebFetch, or WebSearch disabled tools.
- OpenCode policy rejects fixedArgs other than ['acp'] and rejects onePromptPerRun false.
- The new failure code schema accepts all R21 named provider codes and rejects unknown provider codes.
- The contract index exports every schema and type needed by core, adapters, worker, server, and scripts.

Required checks before GREEN:
- pnpm --filter @switchyard/contracts test -- provider-runtime-policy.contract.test.ts
- pnpm --filter @switchyard/contracts typecheck
- git diff --check

Commit your changes before returning GREEN. Return JSON only with status, commit, checks, files_changed, and summary. If blocked, return BLOCKED with exact reason.

- Unknown hosted runtime mode strings still fail closed before policy lookup.

- Claude hosted provider mode maps approval-required and post-start input states to hosted_approval_bridge_unsupported or hosted_input_bridge_unsupported.
- OpenCode hosted provider mode runs one ACP prompt per run, passes only filtered env, and maps permission/input bridge states to hosted_approval_bridge_unsupported or hosted_input_bridge_unsupported.

You are the CEO spec author for the next Switchyard release phase.

Worktree: /Users/vasuyadav/Downloads/Projects/switchyard/.worktrees/post-r11-remaining-20260530/phase-21-r22-hosted-connected-real-tools
Branch: agent/phase-21-r22-hosted-connected-real-tools
Do not merge to root/current branch. Do not use scripts/codex-org.

Context:
- R21 is complete and closed on parent branch: production hosted provider runtime activation for known providers `codex.exec_json`, `claude_code.sdk`, `opencode.acp`; fake-only default; no dashboard/TUI; no public arbitrary execution routes.
- PRODUCT.md says S3/R2 object-store client wiring is already shipped in R13, so do not duplicate it.
- Remaining open gap to address now: hosted/connected-node real-tool execution. R17 shipped local-daemon real tools (`fetch`, `web_search`, `github`, `repo`, command-catalog `shell`) through `POST /tools/invocations` with deny-by-default and approval-by-default policy, but PRODUCT.md still says browser automation and hosted/connected-node real-tool execution are not shipped.

Phase objective:
Write a product spec for Phase 21 / R22 Hosted And Connected-Node Real Tool Execution. The phase should extend the existing R17 tool policy/approval/contracts into hosted/server and connected-node execution paths in a production-safe way.

Required boundaries:
- No dashboard or TUI.
- No public `/exec`, `/shell`, `/process`, `/command`, `/pty`, `/terminal`, or `/sandbox` arbitrary execution routes.
- No generic process/PTY runtime adapters.
- No Cursor/OpenClaw/Paperclip adapters.
- No hosted debate real participants or model judging.
- No hosted approval/input/terminal bridge for provider runtimes beyond existing explicit tool approval records.
- Browser automation remains out of scope unless you can define a no-browser safe placeholder; prefer leaving browser automation out of R22.
- Shell tool may be hosted/connected-node only through the existing command-catalog policy, never arbitrary request-owned commands.
- Hosted/connected-node tools must be disabled by default, explicitly allowlisted, policy-driven, approval-by-default, tenant-scoped, quota/entitlement/audit enforced, redacted, and no-spend/fake by default in tests.

Spec should cover:
- Product goal and non-goals.
- Exact shipped tool set for R22: likely hosted/connected-node `fetch`, `web_search`, `github`, `repo`, and command-catalog `shell` where safe. If a tool should stay local-only, state that explicitly.
- Contracts/API behavior, auth/tenant/quota/audit, side-effect ordering, approval behavior, node assignment/sync behavior, artifact/content storage, logs/events/redaction, readiness/preflight/manifest/canary/docs, OpenAPI boundaries.
- Testing/acceptance with deterministic no-spend fake adapters and no live external calls by default.

Write the spec to `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-01-phase-21-r22-hosted-connected-real-tools.md`, commit it, and return strict JSON with status GREEN, spec_path, phases or task themes, commit hash, and summary.

Revise the Phase 21 / R22 plan for the remaining architect pass 2 blocker only.

Worktree: /Users/vasuyadav/Downloads/Projects/switchyard/.worktrees/post-r11-remaining-20260530/phase-21-r22-hosted-connected-real-tools
Branch: agent/phase-21-r22-hosted-connected-real-tools
Plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-01-phase-21-r22-hosted-connected-real-tools.md
Current revised plan commit: b5cde5146e6d292371247e817093189359ec6f1b

Architect pass 2 remaining required revision:
- Hosted dispatch idempotency is not fully wired through the queue contract. P21-T3 exports ToolDispatchCallback(input ... idempotencyKey), and P21-T4 forwards approvalId/toolInvocationId idempotency key to hosted queue enqueue, but P21-T5 exports ToolQueuePort as enqueueTool(payload: ToolJobPayload) and ToolJobPayload lacks approvalId/idempotencyKey. If enqueue succeeds but route times out before markDispatched, retry can enqueue duplicate jobs.

Required change:
- Add an explicit hosted queue idempotency contract, e.g. `ToolJobPayload.idempotencyKey` or `enqueueTool(payload, { idempotencyKey })`.
- Require BullMQ and memory queues to dedupe by approvalId/toolInvocationId or tool dispatch outbox id.
- Add matching tests for enqueue succeeded + markDispatched timeout + retry producing exactly one queue job.
- Update P21-T5 exports/integration contracts/checks/test cases; update any P21-T3/T4 import/dispatch wording needed so the same idempotency key is consistently threaded.

Commit the plan revision and return strict JSON with status GREEN, commit hash, summary, verification, and implementation_ready true/false.

Blocking findings to fix:

1. HIGH: `apps/server/src/app.ts` `instrumentQueue()` drops hosted tool queue prototype methods.
- `createServerApp()` wraps the real queue in `instrumentQueue()` (`apps/server/src/app.ts:95-103`).
- `instrumentQueue()` rebuilds it with object spread (`apps/server/src/app.ts:578-619`). Since `MemoryRunQueue`/`BullMqRunQueue` implement tool queue methods as class prototype methods, spreading drops `enqueueTool`, `claimTool`, `ackTool`, `failTool`, and `recoverStaleToolClaims`.
- Hosted dispatch then sees `hasToolQueueSupport(queue) === false` (`apps/server/src/app.ts:303-308`), and readiness falsely reports `checks.tools` unavailable (`apps/server/src/readiness.ts:349,428-433`) even with a valid queue.
- Fix by preserving/forwarding tool queue methods when instrumenting the queue. Returning the original queue instance with wrapped methods attached is acceptable, or explicitly bind and forward `enqueueTool`, `claimTool`, `ackTool`, `failTool`, `recoverStaleToolClaims`.
- Add a server-level test enabling tools with valid policy/queue proving `checks.tools.ok === true` and hosted approval dispatch reaches `enqueueTool`.

2. HIGH: mandatory approval list/get/resolve route/app integration coverage is missing.
- Current tests only cover broad route presence/scope and readiness. Add owned route/app integration tests covering:
  - `GET /approvals`
  - `GET /approvals/:id`
  - approval pagination
  - runtime approval denial
  - cross-tenant lookup / no existence leak
  - `POST /approvals/:id/approve` and `POST /approvals/:id/reject` through server wiring
  - approve exactly-once behavior
  - reject terminalization
  - dispatch idempotency-key propagation into queue payload
- Ensure `tools:read` enforcement, ownership-first scoping, and tool-only filtering are covered.

- Server selects only owned, online nodes advertising the required capability and satisfying server-side node policy.
- Tool assignment claim response includes assignment kind `tool`, run, and redacted `toolInvocation` payload with `executionPlanHash`.
- Node revalidates local policy before executing a tool and rejects with `node_policy_denied` on mismatch.
- Connected-node repo executes only read-only local Git inspection under allowed cwd prefixes and fixed argv.
- Connected-node shell executes only node-owned command catalog entries with bounded args and no raw command/env/PTY/terminal request fields.
- Connected-node fetch/search/github use node-owned config and deterministic fake clients in tests, never hosted worker config or hosted credentials.
- Node app constructs `buildNodeToolAdapters(NodeToolAdapterConfig)` from node-owned config/cwd policy/command catalog only; tests reject WorkerConfig-shaped or hosted-credential inputs.
- Browser assignment denies with `browser_tool_unshipped`.
- Event sync accepts `tool.call` and `tool.result` with monotonic sequence and rejects gaps with `event_sync_gap`.
- Artifact sync preserves digest, size, metadata-only/full policy, and rejects digest mismatch or oversized content.
- Assignment complete updates durable invocation only when assignment/run/node/tool ids match.
- Node offline or assignment expiry terminalizes invocation with `tool_assignment_expired` or `tool_node_unavailable`.
- Server never sends GitHub/search tokens, shell env secrets, executable path secrets, or raw catalog contents in assignment payloads.

Integration contracts:
- Export/maintain `NodeClient.claim(nodeId: string, input?: AssignmentClaimRequest) => Promise<AssignmentClaimResponse>`.
- Add/maintain node tool execution path: `executeToolAssignment(input: { assignment: Assignment; run: Run; toolInvocation: ToolInvocation }) => Promise<NodeExecutionResult>`.
- Import from T3 only: `NodeCoordinatorService.createToolAssignment(...)`; do not duplicate its logic.
- Import from T5: `buildNodeToolAdapters(config: NodeToolAdapterConfig, deps?: NodeToolAdapterDeps) => Map<string, ToolAdapter>`.

Required checks before GREEN:
- `pnpm --filter @switchyard/protocol-node test -- node-routes.test.ts`
- `pnpm --filter @switchyard/node test -- node-app.test.ts production-config.test.ts`
- `pnpm --filter @switchyard/protocol-node typecheck`
- `pnpm --filter @switchyard/node typecheck`
- `git diff --check`

Return JSON with status, commit_hash, checks, files_changed, and summary. If blocked by non-owned files or design conflict, return NEEDS_CONTEXT with the exact blocker and smallest required scope expansion.

Current T6 commit: `87817b47ac3a873012686f608ca7d495054ad8cd`

Blocking finding:

HIGH confidence 10: connected-node tool path still executes server-sent `executionPlan` for non-`fake_echo` tools.

Details:
- In `apps/node/src/app.ts` around line 212, `executeToolAssignment()` copies `toolInvocation.input.executionPlan` straight into `adapter.invoke(...)`.
- The adapters trust that plan for process execution:
  - `RepoToolAdapter` runs `plan.gitBinary`, `plan.argv`, and `plan.cwd`.
  - `ShellCatalogToolAdapter` runs `plan.executablePath`, `plan.argv`, `plan.cwd`, and `plan.env`.
- This violates P21-T6 acceptance: connected-node execution must be derived from node-owned config/policy/credentials/command catalog and must not honor request-owned executable/cwd/env policy or server-sent credentials.

Required change:
- Remove node-side dependence on the claimed `executionPlan` for connected-node execution.
- The node should derive repo and shell execution from node-owned configuration (`gitBinary`, shell catalog, node-owned cwd policy, node-owned credentials) plus a sanitized request shape.
- Claim payload should only expose what the node is allowed to consume, such as sanitized `request` plus `executionPlanHash`, not executable/env-bearing plan. The route redaction helper currently only strips `executionPlan.env` for shell; tighten it so raw executable/cwd/env plans are not exposed to nodes for connected execution.
- Add regression tests that send malicious claimed `executionPlan` and prove the node ignores/rejects server-sent `gitBinary`, `executablePath`, `env`, and out-of-policy `cwd`.

Preserve the prior green checks and rerun before returning:
- `pnpm --filter @switchyard/protocol-node test -- node-routes.test.ts`
- `pnpm --filter @switchyard/node test -- node-app.test.ts production-config.test.ts`
- `pnpm --filter @switchyard/protocol-node typecheck`
- `pnpm --filter @switchyard/node typecheck`
- `git diff --check`

Current branch tip: `3eaa2aa67f40e69b67a2737bdfb1486c439b4f60`
Audit report: `/Users/vasuyadav/Downloads/Projects/switchyard/agent-runs/post-r11-remaining-20260530/audit/phase-21-report.md`

Redflag 1: Hosted no-approval tool invocations never dispatch and remain queued forever.
- Area/files: `packages/core/src/services/hosted-tool-service.ts` around lines 153 and 163.
- Auditor repro: `HostedToolService.invoke` with hosted policy `approvalDefault=allow` and `fetch.allowWithoutApproval=true` returned `statusCode=202`, `hasApproval=false`, `invocationStatus=queued`, `dispatches=0`.
- Why blocking: T3 acceptance requires dispatchable work to proceed through hosted/core dispatch path. With allow-without-approval, the invocation is admitted but no approval is created and dispatch is never called.
- Required fix: no-approval hosted invocations must dispatch immediately through the same idempotent hosted dispatch path, with durable outbox/idempotency semantics preserved and quota rollback behavior preserved on persistence/dispatch failures. Add targeted tests covering the no-approval allowWithoutApproval path and no duplicate dispatch on repeated/idempotent invoke.

Redflag 2: Explicit hosted-to-connected-node tool offload is rejected after admission.
- Area/file: `packages/core/src/services/node-coordinator-service.ts` around line 123.
- Auditor repro: `NodeCoordinatorService.createToolAssignment` rejected a hosted run with `metadata.allowToolPlacementOffload=true` with code `node_policy_denied`, message `Run is not eligible for connected-node tool assignment`.
- Why blocking: Spec/T3 acceptance allow target placement mismatch when run metadata explicitly allows tool offload. Hosted admission accepts that contract, but assignment creation rejects it, breaking hosted-to-node execution.
- Required fix: allow `createToolAssignment` for hosted runs only when explicit metadata allowToolPlacementOffload is true, while continuing to reject unmarked hosted runs and all unsafe placement mismatches. Add targeted tests for allowed offload and denied unmarked hosted offload.

Stay tightly scoped to T3/core files and tests where possible. Do not revert other task commits. Before returning GREEN, commit your revision and rerun at least:
- `pnpm --filter @switchyard/core test -- hosted-tool-policy.test.ts tool-approval-dispatch.test.ts tool-router-local-compat.test.ts`

- `git diff --check`

Reviewer returned NEEDS_REVISION on your audit-redflag fix. Continue in same worktree/branch and preserve context.

Worktree: `/Users/vasuyadav/Downloads/Projects/switchyard/.worktrees/post-r11-remaining-20260530/phase-21-r22-hosted-connected-real-tools`
Branch: `agent/phase-21-r22-hosted-connected-real-tools`
Current revision commit: `dfc2063452d599a54c2f3cf9942a6169361fa4dc`

Blocking findings to fix:

1. HIGH confidence 9: no-approval idempotent recovery can dispatch without an active quota reservation.
- Initial allow-without-approval admission reserves quota, persists invocation, then dispatches. If dispatch fails, catch rolls back both reservations.
- A later retry with same idempotency key returns the existing invocation and calls `recoverNoApprovalDispatch()` before `reservePostPolicyQuota()`, so durable outbox retry can enqueue/assign real tool work after quota was released.
- This violates quota ordering: dispatchable work must have hourly and active tool quota reserved; quota-store failures must fail closed before dispatch.
Required fix: ensure no-approval outbox recovery cannot dispatch without quota. Either keep quota reservation when persisted invocation/outbox remains retryable, or re-run `reservePostPolicyQuota()` on existing-idempotency recovery before `recoverNoApprovalDispatch()` and roll it back on recovery failure. Add regression test where first no-approval dispatch fails after persistence, then idempotent retry succeeds only with a valid reservation.

2. HIGH confidence 9: existing-idempotency no-approval recovery uses execution-plan hash from current retry request, not persisted invocation/outbox.
- Repro: first dispatch fails for `https://example.com/first`; retry same idempotency key with `https://example.com/second`; second dispatch carries original invocation request URL but hash for second URL, while outbox still stores first hash.
- `apps/server/src/app.ts` passes this hash into tool queue payload, so dispatch can emit an executionPlanHash that does not describe the invocation.
Required fix: for existing no-approval idempotency recovery, derive dispatch hash from persisted invocation input or existing outbox record, not incoming retry request. Prefer rejecting mismatched idempotency-key payloads with a named conflict. Add test covering failed first dispatch followed by same idempotency key with changed input.

Keep the offload fix intact. Rerun and report:
- `pnpm --filter @switchyard/core test -- hosted-tool-policy.test.ts tool-approval-dispatch.test.ts tool-router-local-compat.test.ts`
- `pnpm --filter @switchyard/core typecheck`
- `git diff --check`

Commit before returning GREEN.

Reviewer returned NEEDS_REVISION for P22-T5. Continue in the same worktree/branch and preserve your context.

Worktree: `/Users/vasuyadav/Downloads/Projects/switchyard/.worktrees/post-r11-remaining-20260530/phase-22-r23-hosted-runtime-bridges`
Branch: `agent/phase-22-r23-hosted-runtime-bridges`
Current T5 commit: `aee78635548c6c9584a78c6d11c6f0d466147f95`

Blocking findings:

1. HIGH confidence 10: hosted approval resolution is not exactly-once under concurrency.
- In `packages/adapters/src/claude-code/claude-code-adapter.ts` around line 212, token presence is checked before `await stored.clientSession.resolveApproval(...)`, and token is deleted afterward.
- Two concurrent `send(...approval_resolution...)` calls can both observe the token as pending and both call the client. Reviewer verified `resolveApprovalCalls=2`.
Required fix: make approval resolution single-flight per token. Remove/reserve the token before awaiting the client call, or add an in-flight token guard. Second concurrent resolution should fail closed with `runtime_approval_pause_not_active` and must not call client resolve twice. Add regression test with delayed resolve proving exactly one client call.

2. HIGH confidence 10: named failure mapping leaks raw client error text.
- Around line 192, `AdapterProtocolError.details.error` includes raw client error messages for send and approval-resolution failures.
- Reviewer verified secret-bearing strings like `api-secret leaked` and `approval-token secret-value` are exposed.
Required fix: keep named reason codes (`claude_input_send_failed`, `claude_approval_resolution_failed`) but sanitize or omit provider/client error text before attaching details. Use a redacted low-cardinality marker if needed. Add tests proving secret-bearing error strings are not exposed.

Rerun:
- `pnpm --filter @switchyard/adapters test -- claude-code-adapter.test.ts claude-code-cli-client.test.ts claude-code-transcript-bounds.test.ts`
- `pnpm --filter @switchyard/testkit test -- fake-claude-code-client`
- `pnpm --filter @switchyard/adapters typecheck`
- `pnpm --filter @switchyard/testkit typecheck`
- `git diff --check`

Commit revision before returning GREEN.

- ACP held request TTL, approval.expiresAt, and command.expiresAt share an explicit deadline or strict ordering; resolution after expiry returns `acp_permission_request_expired` without JSON-RPC write.
- Worker apply re-reads run/session/approval and verifies ownership before adapter dispatch.

- `listByRun` remains storage-test-only and must not appear in this core port.

Worktree: `/Users/vasuyadav/Downloads/Projects/switchyard/.worktrees/post-r11-remaining-20260530/phase-22-r23-hosted-runtime-bridges`
Branch: `agent/phase-22-r23-hosted-runtime-bridges`
Current T3 commit: `e028fbc`

Blocking findings:

1. HIGH confidence 10: quota lifecycle is not durable across server -> worker boundary.
- Reservation IDs are only in in-memory `commandReservations` (`hosted-runtime-bridge-service.ts` around lines 118, 220, 381).
- Finalization only uses `finalizeCommandReservations` around lines 525 and 777-780.
- In real architecture, server admits command and worker completes/fails/reconciles it in another process, so worker has empty map and never finalizes active/hourly quota reservations.
- `expireStale()` also expires queued commands without quota finalization.
Required fix: Make quota reservation state durable/recoverable by worker/reconciler, or finalize quota from persisted command state instead of process memory. Add tests using separate service instances to model server and worker for complete, fail, expire, and stale-claim recovery.

2. HIGH confidence 10: worker dispatch reconstructs from redacted payload instead of original normalized payload.
- `redactForStorage()` stores masked `content` / `message` around lines 873-899.
- `payloadForWorkerDispatch()` reuses those redacted fields for `sendInput` around lines 901-926.
- `claimAndApplyNext()` hashes reconstructed payload and compares against original raw hash around lines 500-507.
- For secret-bearing input, this either sends redacted content to runtime or fails with payload mismatch. Required behavior: worker applies admitted input while storage remains redacted.
Required fix: Preserve a worker-dispatchable original normalized payload without leaking it through logs/audit/errors/tests, and validate its hash against stored raw-payload hash before dispatch. Add test: admit secret-bearing input, worker sends original text exactly once, storage remains redacted.

3. HIGH confidence 10: `createWorkerRuntimeApproval()` creates approval row but never attaches ownership.
- The dependency declares `preflight.attachOwnership`, but method only `approvals.create()` and returns around lines 409-434.
- Hosted routes depend on ownership records to list/resolve approvals.
Required fix: After creating runtime approval, attach ownership for `resourceType: 'approval'` before it is visible/resolvable; fail closed if attachment cannot be persisted. Add focused test that ownership attach failure leaves no pending visible/orphan approval.

Keep `listByRun` out of the core port. Preserve existing checks. Rerun:
- `pnpm --filter @switchyard/core test -- hosted-runtime-bridge-service.test.ts runtime-approval-session-r16.test.ts core.test.ts`
- `pnpm --filter @switchyard/core typecheck`
- `git diff --check`

Commit revision before returning GREEN.

Blocking findings:

1. HIGH confidence 9: Runtime approval resolve requests do not forward the caller's `Idempotency-Key` into `HostedRuntimeBridgeService.resolveRuntimeApproval`.
- `POST /runs/:id/input` does this correctly, but `/approvals/:id/approve` and `/approvals/:id/reject` do not.
- Same-header replays are not keyed by caller idempotency, so same key with different `message`/`answers` can bypass payload-mismatch handling and collapse to `approval_not_pending`.
Required fix:
- Read `Idempotency-Key` on `/approvals/:id/approve` and `/approvals/:id/reject`.
- Pass it to `resolveRuntimeApproval`.
- Add route tests for same-key same-decision replay and same-key different-payload mismatch.

2. HIGH confidence 9: Server wires bridge dispatch payloads into process-local `Map` via `createInMemoryBridgeCommandPayloadStore()` in `apps/server/src/app.ts`.
- Durable command row stores only redacted payload metadata.
- Worker can apply only if it can reload raw payload from same payload store.
- In production server and hosted worker are separate processes; a server-local map makes admitted commands non-dispatchable outside single-process tests.
Required fix:
- Replace server-local in-memory payload store with a worker-readable shared mechanism, or fail closed/defer bridge admission until a shared payload store exists.
- The server must not persist raw bridge payloads only in private process memory when worker is separate.
- If this requires a narrow T3/core or T2/storage scope expansion, return NEEDS_CONTEXT with exact file/scope and proposed contract. Do not hack around it with a fake single-process assumption.

Current T8 commit: `aac0db1`

Blocking findings:

1. HIGH confidence 9: `reconcileHostedBridgeSessionOwnership()` rewrites `session.state.hostedWorkerId` for every active supported hosted run, even when already owned by a different live worker.
- This defeats `HostedRuntimeBridgeService.claimAndApplyNext()` ownership checks; any worker that ticks last can seize another worker's live session.
Required fix:
- Do not overwrite `hostedWorkerId` during broad startup reconciliation.
- Only stamp ownership when this worker actually starts/creates the hosted session, or when reclaiming a session provably unowned/recoverable under a durable rule.
- Preserve another worker's ownership and fail closed instead of stealing it.
- Add a multi-worker test proving one worker cannot claim/apply commands for another worker's live hosted session.

2. HIGH confidence 10: R23 hosted input/approval bridge still has no real shared payload handoff between server and worker.
- Server currently uses `createUnavailableBridgeCommandPayloadStore()` and every admission path fails with `hosted_runtime_bridge_store_unavailable`.
- Worker requires injected `bridgeCommandPayloads`; tests only assert fail-closed when missing.
- This does not ship a usable hosted input/approval bridge.
Required fix:
- Wire a real shared command payload store that both server admission and worker claim/apply use by default.
- Verify an end-to-end hosted input/approval flow succeeds across process boundaries.
- If the clean implementation requires narrow scope beyond P22-T8 owned files, you are authorized to use the smallest needed cross-task files, but keep it focused and list exactly why. Likely candidates: `apps/server/src/app.ts`, `apps/server/test/...`, maybe a small core helper/interface or storage-backed payload store if already implied by existing dependencies.
- Do not fake this with a single-process map; server and worker must be able to use the same durable/shared backing.

Rerun at minimum:
- `pnpm --filter @switchyard/worker test -- hosted-worker.test.ts production-worker-readiness.test.ts`
- `pnpm --filter @switchyard/worker typecheck`
- `pnpm --filter @switchyard/core test -- hosted-worker-service.test.ts hosted-runtime-bridge-service.test.ts`
- Add/rerun any server/core/storage tests touched by the shared payload store fix.
- `git diff --check`

- Model judging should be through an internal bounded judge runner path using existing run/runtime contracts, not a new public arbitrary model route. Live judging requires explicit spend confirmation and canary opt-in.
- Preserve hard debate stop limits, quota/audit/ownership for hosted/server placement, artifact/report traceability, event/message persistence, and no tenant leakage.
- Keep unshipped: dashboard/TUI, public arbitrary exec/shell/process/pty/terminal/sandbox routes, hosted codex.interactive/session resume, AgentField/Generic HTTP bridges unless explicitly still fail-closed, Cursor/OpenClaw/Paperclip, browser automation, hosted repo, generic process/PTY adapters, managed SaaS/payment/OAuth.

Reviewer returned NEEDS_REVISION for P23-T3. Please make a narrow fix in the same worktree and same owned files, commit it, and report checks.

Finding:
- `packages/core/src/services/debate-output.ts` around the ownership check: `extractDebateRuntimeOutput()` currently fails on the first mismatched `runtime.output` event instead of selecting the matching persisted output. The helper signature accepts a debate-scoped event list; callers may pass unrelated participant/judge outputs in the same list. One unrelated `runtime.output` followed by a valid matching output currently returns `debate_participant_output_unowned` before reaching the valid event.

Required change:
- Filter `runtime.output` events down to those matching expected `debateId` and `childRunKey`, then apply missing/blank/too-large validation to the latest matching event.
- Preserve fail-closed behavior for an output event for the expected runId whose debate/key metadata mismatches, if `expected.runId` is supplied. Otherwise mixed debate-scoped event lists should not block valid matching output extraction.
- Add a regression test with mixed persisted debate events where an unrelated output appears before a valid later matching output and extraction succeeds.

Checks:
- `pnpm --filter @switchyard/core exec vitest run test/debate-real-runtime.test.ts`
- `pnpm --filter @switchyard/core typecheck`
- `git diff --check`

Commit before responding.

Findings:

1) Stale exhausted/invalid debate claims are not propagated to debate terminal failure or active_debates quota cleanup.
- Current recovery path logs recovered/exhausted/invalid counters and proceeds. When recoverStaleClaims exhausts a debate job, worker does not fail associated debate or finalize quota.
- Required: propagate exhausted/invalid stale-claim results back to debate state before returning from processDebateJobs: mark affected debate terminal failed with named reason code (e.g. debate_execution_attempts_exhausted / hosted_debate_worker_unavailable as appropriate) and invoke finalizeActiveDebateQuota exactly once. Strengthen stale-claim test to assert terminal debate state and quota finalization, not just job state.

2) Worker quota finalization selects oldest active reservation by project scope instead of the reservation for the completed debate.
- `createActiveDebateQuotaFinalizer()` currently queries oldest reserved `active_debates` row by account/tenant/project. With multiple active debates, finishing debate B can finalize debate A's reservation.
- Required: persist/carry the specific active_debates reservation id on the debate or debate execution job at admission time, and finalize that exact reservation in the worker. Since T8 owned files are worker-only, use the reservation id if already present in debate/job metadata from server/T6, and add a safe fail-closed or explicit TODO/deferred only if server-side propagation is impossible in this task. Prefer using existing job/debate fields if available. Add tests proving the exact reservation id is used, not oldest.

4. Verify server/worker readiness includes independent `payload_store`, wrapper_config, wrapper_bridge_capability, worker claim/session reconciliation/approval sender, and production preflight consumes the default real diagnostics path.
5. Verify hosted debate admits wrapper participants only with hosted placement, `realRuntimeOptIn`, catalog/provider match, provider activation/spend gates, bridge readiness, and blocks `wait=1` real participants before side effects.
6. Verify production preflight/canary remain no-spend by default and live provider bridge canary fails before fetch/provider dispatch without spend confirmation.
7. Verify docs truth is current and all non-goals remain explicit: no dashboard/TUI, hosted `codex.interactive`, Codex hosted input/approval bridge, browser automation, hosted repo execution, generic process/PTY adapters, public arbitrary execution routes, public model judge routes, managed SaaS/billing/OAuth/SSO/SCIM.
8. Verify no new public route family was added. Note: PRODUCT.md local route list contains `POST /approvals` as local middleware route; hosted docs/OpenAPI should still prove hosted `POST /approvals` absent and wrapper approvals reuse list/get/approve/reject.

Run any focused checks needed. Write a concise audit report with:
- status GREEN or NEEDS_REVISION
- summary
- checks run/reused
- redflags (if any, with file/line/severity/confidence/required fix)
- deferred concerns (if any)
- non-blocking observations

Return GREEN only if the phase can be closed. If NEEDS_REVISION, do not soften it; list exact redflags.
